channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Massachusetts Democrat suggests Trump’s TikTok support stems from his popularity on the app
item.description: Rep. Jake Auchincloss (D-Mass.) suggested that President-elect Trump's opposition to a TikTok ban comes from his popularity on the app and the video-sharing platform lobby’s influence on his inner circle. “With Donald Trump, it’s always some combination of two things, Jim.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: DJIin 2024: the drone-maker's highs and lows, plus what to expect in 2025
item.description: It's been quite a year for DJI – we've looked back at all of its best launches, plus what to expect from the drone king in what could be a turbulent 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft backed a tiny hardware startup that just launched its first AI processor that does inference without GPU or expensive HBM memory and a key Nvidia partner is collaborating with it
item.description: Microsoft-backed d-Matrix launches Corsair, an AI processor offering efficient inference, GPU independence, and support for generative AI applications.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Android 15's new Private Space feature puts an end to app snooping
item.description: Android 15's latest feature, Private Space, is like a private vault helping to keep your personal or sensitive apps away from prying eyes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers, former officials weigh in on TikTok ban as case heads to Supreme Court
item.description: Top lawmakers, First Amendment advocacy groups and President-elect Trump weighed in Friday on a law that could ban TikTok in the U.S., as the Supreme Court prepares to take up the case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: New York state government to monitor its use of AI under a new law
item.description: New York state government agencies will have to conduct reviews and publish reports that detail how they’re using artificial intelligence software, under a new law signed by Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Critical warning from Microsoft: .NET install domains changing
item.description: Users who expected to be able to use the resources of the Azure content delivery network (CDN) from Edgio until November 2025, as previously announced, had a not-so-festive “gift” this week – the CDN is now shutting down on Jan. 15, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: An online debate over foreign workers in tech shows tensions in Trump's political coalition
item.description: An online spat between factions of Donald Trump’s supporters over immigration and the tech industry has thrown internal divisions in the president-elect’s political movement into public display.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexico tests cellphone app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico is developing a cellphone app that will allow migrants to warn relatives and local consulates if they think they are about to be detained by the U.S. immigration department, a senior official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pedal Ahead founder is out as California e-bike program operator, but questions and reporting discrepancies persist
item.description: The nonprofit was the subject of a criminal investigation earlier this year and a civil probe by the Air Resources Board, according to records.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Mcdonald, The Mercury News

item.title: Nearly all companies expect a major outage in 2025
item.description: After 2024's CrowdStrike-induced outage, IT leaders are more worried about “when” the next outage will be, not “if” there’ll be one.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Chinese competitor to AMD and Intel claims its 32-core processor is as powerful as a 2021 Intel Xeon 32-core 'Ice Lake' CPU; a 64-core version is expected to be launched in 2025
item.description: Loongson's 3C6000 series processors rival Intel Xeon chips, showcasing China's semiconductor progress.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Undersea power cable linking Finland and Estonia hit by outage, prompting investigation
item.description: FRANKFURT, Germany (AP) — A power cable linking Finland and Estonia under the Baltic Sea suffered an outage, prompting an investigation, Finnish Prime Minister Petteri Orpo said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The top Windows PC tips of 2024
item.description: The end of the year means it’s time to reflect on what’s really important in life: friends, family — and Windows PC tips. That may sound silly, but I’ve always believed it’s true.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Python in 2024: Faster, more powerful, and more popular than ever
item.description: Over the course of 2024, Python has proven again and again why it’s one of the most popular, useful, and promising programming languages out there.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: WinGet: The best way to keep Windows apps updated
item.description: For more than two years, I’ve been working with WinGet daily to monitor and maintain the apps on my Windows 10 and 11 PCs. For those not already in the know, WinGet is the built-in, command-line interface to Microsoft’s Windows Package Manager service.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fake AI-generated reviews are all over the internet, research shows
item.description: The emergence of generative artificial intelligence tools that allow people to efficiently produce novel and detailed online reviews with almost no work has put merchants, service providers and consumers in uncharted territory, watchdog groups and researchers say.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Trump administration’s AI team comes into focus, as agencies reach 1,700 AI use cases
item.description: Federal CIO Clare Martorana says the Trump administration should consider a shared-services model to accelerate the use of AI across agencies. The post Trump administration’s AI team comes into focus, as agencies reach 1,700 AI use cases first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: 5 network automation startups to watch
item.description: To meet digital transformation initiatives, businesses across the economy are seeking ways to automate network operations. According to a report from Mordor Intelligence, the network automation market should top $25 billion by the end of 2024, expanding to more than $71 billion in 2030.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Top 5 mistakes that could expose your financial data to cybercriminals
item.description: There are common habits and oversights that could leave your financial information vulnerable to cybercriminals, according to Kurt the CyberGuy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe aims to fly closer to the sun like never before
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A NASA spacecraft aims to fly closer to the sun than any object sent before. The Parker Solar Probe was launched in 2018 to get a close-up look at the sun. Since then, it has flown straight through the sun's corona: the outer atmosphere visible during a total solar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
